sanskrit:
मम योनिर्महद् ब्रह्म तस्मिन् गर्भं दधाम्यहम्।
संभवः सर्वभूतानां ततो भवति भारत।।
transliteration:
mama yonir mahad brahma tasmin garbhaṁ dadhāmy aham |
saṁbhavaḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ tato bhavati bhārata ||
literal_meaning: My womb is the great Brahman; in that I place the seed; from this the birth of all beings becomes possible, O Bhārata.
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that every experience—thought, feeling, or body-state—is born when consciousness (the living entity) is ‘impregnated’ into the vast field of material nature. The Self does not create the mind from nothing; it fertilizes an already-existing matrix of potential forms. Psychological life is therefore co-creation: innate intelligence meets the raw substance of memory, sensation, and social conditioning.

shadow: Fear of being swallowed by the mother

psychological_state: Arjuna feels both wonder and vertigo at realizing that his own mind is not an isolated ego-factory but a gestation inside a larger maternal field. The verse invites him to shift from ‘I must produce my life’ to ‘I participate in a life that is already being conceived through me.’
trigger_scenario: A client in therapy suddenly sees that every anxious thought is not ‘mine’ but a sprout in the communal soil of family scripts, media, and neurochemistry; the insight is simultaneously relieving (‘I’m not broken’) and unsettling (‘Then who am I?’).
anchor_text: Consciousness seeds the mind-field; every thought is co-born, not self-made.
